issuer: Indian Computer Emergency Response Team, Ministry of Electronics and IT
applicability: All entities operating in India including service providers, intermediaries, data centres, government organisations

directives.title: Mandatory Cyber Incident Reporting
directives.description: Any cyber incident must be reported to CERT-In within 6 hours of noticing it or being informed about it.
covered_incident_types: Defacement of website or intrusion into a website
Malicious code attacks (virus, worm, Trojan, ransomware, spyware)
Attacks on critical infrastructure, SCADA, OT systems
Attacks on Internet of Things (IoT) devices
evidence: Internal incident log with detection timestamp

directives.title: Synchronisation with NTP
directives.description: All ICT infrastructure must synchronise system clocks with the National Physical Laboratory (NPL) NTP server or NIC NTP server.
directives.requirement: Logs and timestamps used in investigations must be correlated with NPL/NIC time sources.

directives.title: Log Retention
directives.description: All ICT systems must maintain logs for a minimum of 180 days (6 months). Logs must be available within India.

directives.title: Designated Point of Contact
directives.description: All entities must designate a Point of Contact (PoC) to coordinate with CERT-In. PoC details must be registered on the CERT-In portal.
directives.requirement: PoC must be reachable 24x7. Any change in PoC must be notified within 7 days.

directives.title: VPN and Virtual Asset Provider Logs
directives.description: VPN service providers, Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs), and Virtual Private Server (VPS) providers must maintain logs of all transactions for 5 years.
directives.kyc_requirement: Validated customer details (KYC) must be maintained for all users.

directives.title: Vulnerability Disclosure and Threat Intelligence
directives.description: Entities must share information with CERT-In on cyber threats, vulnerabilities, and incidents as required.
directives.requirement: Entities must implement a vulnerability disclosure policy and act on CERT-In advisories within specified timelines.
